title: Validate candidate rule sets against private evidence
description: Evaluate candidate normalization and rule-set versions against owner-only representative and temporal hold-out evidence before activation or broad apply. Implements G6 and UC-CLASSIFY-004.
rationale: A deterministic rule can still be consistently wrong; private held-out evidence is the activation safety gate.
story_want: validate candidate rules against representative private evidence
story_so_that: unsafe or brittle behavior cannot be activated or applied broadly
acceptance_criteria: Given a candidate normalization or rule-set version and an owner-only corpus, when validation starts, then the run records the candidate, corpus, expected-outcome, category-catalogue, projection-contract, and normalization fingerprints.

Given a completed validation run, when its report is returned, then every corpus row is accounted for and aggregate totals report suggestions, no-suggestions, conflicts, stale rows, drift canaries, and incorrect-application canaries.
Given any missing or stale required fingerprint, when activation is attempted, then CLASSIFY rejects activation without changing the active Rule Set Version.
Given any incorrect-application canary, when activation is attempted, then CLASSIFY rejects activation without changing the active Rule Set Version.
Given any unexplained incompatible-category conflict in required evidence, when activation is attempted, then CLASSIFY rejects activation without changing the active Rule Set Version.
Given an unavailable private corpus, when an existing active rule set is evaluated, then CLASSIFY may use that unchanged active version.
Given an unavailable private corpus, when a new rule or normalization version is activated, then CLASSIFY rejects activation without changing the active Rule Set Version.
Given an unavailable private corpus, when broad-apply authorization is requested, then CLASSIFY rejects authorization before LEDGER mutation.

Given a validation report, when it is persisted or exported, then it contains corpus fingerprints and aggregate outcomes but no raw transaction description, normalized token, financial value, or expected-outcome row.
security_criteria: Private evidence remains outside Git and is readable only by the trusted owner OS identity.
